title: podman stop command fails to properly stop container
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
When issuing podman stop to a container command hangs for a while and than works.  During that time podman ps also hangs.  It looks like podman ignores SIGTERM and is killed by SIGKILL upon 10 seconds timeout.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  Run container without detaching
```
podman run  --rm --net=host --no-hosts=true --name=httpd-echo --pull=always hashicorp/http-echo:latest -listen ':8080' -text 'Hello World'
```
2.  Stop container
```
podman stop httpd-echo
```
**Describe the results you received:**
On the stderr / stdout of attached terminal I get

`[ERR] Unknown signal terminated`

Eventually, after 10 seconds,  I see podman stop returning an id of stopped container
The same situation is with detached container but ERR is in the logs

**Describe the results you expected:**
No error, container is stopped quickly with command returning a stopped container id


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.9.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  compiler: gc
  gitCommit: ""
  goVersion: go1.10.1
  podmanVersion: 1.9.2
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.14.8

  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.16, commit: '
  cpus: 4
  distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: alex3.xcastlabs.net
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 4.15.0-62-generic
  memFree: 1206530048
  memTotal: 4136144896
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 2155h 55m 11.91s (Approximately 89.79 days)

  search:
  - docker.io

    number: 2

    stopped: 0

  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 1.9.2~2 amd64 [installed]
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

KVM virtual machine and physical hardware server

comments.body: Are you sure that message is not coming from the app inside the container? That doesn't seem to match the format of the error logs Podman itself prints.

I just verified it is indeed coming from inside the container.  The 10 seconds is a timeout.  It looks like the app in your program catches the first signal.  After a 10 second timeout, podman kills it.

Yes you are right, looks like they wait for interrupt to kill the server 
```
signalCh := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
	signal.Notify(signalCh, os.Interrupt)

	// Wait for interrupt
	<-signalCh
```
Tested with nginx an it all good, sorry for jumping to conclusions.
Now, I wonder though why podman ps is hanging when I issue podman stop against this container in different terminal.  It looks like podman is waiting for one container to die.
because while podman is waiting for the timeout, it has the container locked.  you'd be better suited to do a `podman stop -t0 httpd-echo` in this case.
I think I understand.  Thank you all.
